  "text": "The Kafka broker-side SASL authentication implementation is structured around a central server authenticator that manages the entire authentication lifecycle. The key components are:\n\n1. **Server Authenticator Core**: SaslServerAuthenticator (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java) implements the Authenticator interface and manages the overall authentication state machine. It handles the SASL handshake protocol, mechanism negotiation, and delegates to mechanism-specific SaslServer implementations. The createSaslServer() method selects the appropriate mechanism implementation based on the negotiated SASL mechanism.\n\n2. **Authentication Context Lifecycle**: SaslAuthenticationContext (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/SaslAuthenticationContext.java) encapsulates the authentication context during the authentication process, wrapping javax.security.sasl.SaslServer and providing security protocol, client address, and listener information.\n\n3. **PLAIN Mechanism**: The PLAIN mechanism is implemented through PlainSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServer.java), which implements the javax.security.sasl.SaslServer interface. The PlainSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServerProvider.java) registers the PlainSaslServerFactory that creates PLAIN server instances. PlainServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainServerCallbackHandler.java) handles the JAAS callbacks for credential validation using the user_<username> format from JAAS configuration.\n\n4. **SCRAM Mechanism**: The SCRAM mechanism implementation includes ScramSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServer.java) with ScramSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServerProvider.java) handling both SCRAM-SHA-256 and SCRAM-SHA-512 variants via ScramMechanism (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramMechanism.java). ScramServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramServerCallbackHandler.java) manages credential and token validation using CredentialCache, while ScramFormatter (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramFormatter.java) handles SCRAM message formatting and credential generation.\n\n5. **GSSAPI/Kerberos Mechanism**: Kerberos authentication is handled through KerberosLogin (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosLogin.java) and KerberosClientCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosClientCallbackHandler.java). Server-side GSSAPI uses Java's built-in GSSAPI provider. KerberosName (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosName.java) and KerberosShortNamer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosShortNamer.java) handle principal name mapping according to JAAS rules defined in KerberosRule (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosRule.java).\n\n6. **OAuthBearer Mechanism**: OAuthBearerSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServer.java) implements OAuth 2.0 token-based authentication with OAuthBearerSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServerProvider.java) registering the OAuthBearerSaslServerFactory.\n\n7. **Mechanism Selection Infrastructure**: SaslChannelBuilder (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java) orchestrates the creation of server-side authentication components. It loads JAAS contexts for enabled mechanisms, creates appropriate callback handlers based on the mechanism type, and builds the authenticator. ChannelBuilders (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java) provides factory methods for creating SASL channel builders for both client and server modes.\n\n8. **Callback Handler Infrastructure**: SaslServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerCallbackHandler.java) provides default callback handling for all SASL mechanisms, while mechanism-specific handlers (PlainServerCallbackHandler, ScramServerCallbackHandler) override behavior for their respective mechanisms. AuthenticateCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/AuthenticateCallbackHandler.java) is the interface that all callback handlers must implement.\n\n9. **JAAS Context Management**: JaasContext (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java) and JaasConfig (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasConfig.java) manage JAAS configuration loading and context creation for both static and dynamic JAAS configurations. LoginManager (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/LoginManager.java) manages the lifecycle of JAAS login modules.\n\n10. **Credential Storage**: CredentialCache (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/CredentialCache.java) provides in-memory caching of credentials for SCRAM mechanisms, supporting dynamic credential updates via broker admin operations.\n\nThe authentication flow begins when a client connects to a SASL listener. SaslChannelBuilder builds the server-side channel with appropriate JAAS contexts for all enabled mechanisms. SaslServerAuthenticator receives the initial SASL handshake request, extracts the client's preferred mechanism, creates a SaslServer instance via the appropriate mechanism provider, and proceeds with the SASL token exchange until authentication succeeds or fails. Throughout this process, the mechanism-specific SaslServer implementations handle the cryptographic and validation logic, with callback handlers bridging to the broker's credential stores and authorization rules."
}

  "text": "The Kafka broker-side SASL authentication implementation is structured around a central server authenticator that manages the entire authentication lifecycle. The key components are:\n\n1. **Server Authenticator Core**: SaslServerAuthenticator (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java) implements the Authenticator interface and manages the overall authentication state machine. It handles the SASL handshake protocol, mechanism negotiation, and delegates to mechanism-specific SaslServer implementations. The createSaslServer() method selects the appropriate mechanism implementation based on the negotiated SASL mechanism.\n\n2. **Authentication Context Lifecycle**: SaslAuthenticationContext (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/SaslAuthenticationContext.java) encapsulates the authentication context during the authentication process, wrapping javax.security.sasl.SaslServer and providing security protocol, client address, and listener information.\n\n3. **PLAIN Mechanism**: The PLAIN mechanism is implemented through PlainSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServer.java), which implements the javax.security.sasl.SaslServer interface. The PlainSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServerProvider.java) registers the PlainSaslServerFactory that creates PLAIN server instances. PlainServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainServerCallbackHandler.java) handles the JAAS callbacks for credential validation using the user_<username> format from JAAS configuration.\n\n4. **SCRAM Mechanism**: The SCRAM mechanism implementation includes ScramSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServer.java) with ScramSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServerProvider.java) handling both SCRAM-SHA-256 and SCRAM-SHA-512 variants via ScramMechanism (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramMechanism.java). ScramServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramServerCallbackHandler.java) manages credential and token validation using CredentialCache, while ScramFormatter (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramFormatter.java) handles SCRAM message formatting and credential generation.\n\n5. **GSSAPI/Kerberos Mechanism**: Kerberos authentication is handled through KerberosLogin (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosLogin.java) and KerberosClientCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosClientCallbackHandler.java). Server-side GSSAPI uses Java's built-in GSSAPI provider. KerberosName (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosName.java) and KerberosShortNamer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosShortNamer.java) handle principal name mapping according to JAAS rules defined in KerberosRule (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosRule.java).\n\n6. **OAuthBearer Mechanism**: OAuthBearerSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServer.java) implements OAuth 2.0 token-based authentication with OAuthBearerSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServerProvider.java) registering the OAuthBearerSaslServerFactory.\n\n7. **Mechanism Selection Infrastructure**: SaslChannelBuilder (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java) orchestrates the creation of server-side authentication components. It loads JAAS contexts for enabled mechanisms, creates appropriate callback handlers based on the mechanism type, and builds the authenticator. ChannelBuilders (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java) provides factory methods for creating SASL channel builders for both client and server modes.\n\n8. **Callback Handler Infrastructure**: SaslServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerCallbackHandler.java) provides default callback handling for all SASL mechanisms, while mechanism-specific handlers (PlainServerCallbackHandler, ScramServerCallbackHandler) override behavior for their respective mechanisms. AuthenticateCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/AuthenticateCallbackHandler.java) is the interface that all callback handlers must implement.\n\n9. **JAAS Context Management**: JaasContext (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java) and JaasConfig (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasConfig.java) manage JAAS configuration loading and context creation for both static and dynamic JAAS configurations. LoginManager (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/LoginManager.java) manages the lifecycle of JAAS login modules.\n\n10. **Credential Storage**: CredentialCache (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/CredentialCache.java) provides in-memory caching of credentials for SCRAM mechanisms, supporting dynamic credential updates via broker admin operations.\n\nThe authentication flow begins when a client connects to a SASL listener. SaslChannelBuilder builds the server-side channel with appropriate JAAS contexts for all enabled mechanisms. SaslServerAuthenticator receives the initial SASL handshake request, extracts the client's preferred mechanism, creates a SaslServer instance via the appropriate mechanism provider, and proceeds with the SASL token exchange until authentication succeeds or fails. Throughout this process, the mechanism-specific SaslServer implementations handle the cryptographic and validation logic, with callback handlers bridging to the broker's credential stores and authorization rules."

  "text": "The Kafka broker-side SASL authentication implementation is structured around a central server authenticator that manages the entire authentication lifecycle. The key components are:\n\n1. **Server Authenticator Core**: SaslServerAuthenticator (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java) implements the Authenticator interface and manages the overall authentication state machine. It handles the SASL handshake protocol, mechanism negotiation, and delegates to mechanism-specific SaslServer implementations. The createSaslServer() method selects the appropriate mechanism implementation based on the negotiated SASL mechanism.\n\n2. **Authentication Context Lifecycle**: SaslAuthenticationContext (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/SaslAuthenticationContext.java) encapsulates the authentication context during the authentication process, wrapping javax.security.sasl.SaslServer and providing security protocol, client address, and listener information.\n\n3. **PLAIN Mechanism**: The PLAIN mechanism is implemented through PlainSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServer.java), which implements the javax.security.sasl.SaslServer interface. The PlainSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServerProvider.java) registers the PlainSaslServerFactory that creates PLAIN server instances. PlainServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainServerCallbackHandler.java) handles the JAAS callbacks for credential validation using the user_<username> format from JAAS configuration.\n\n4. **SCRAM Mechanism**: The SCRAM mechanism implementation includes ScramSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServer.java) with ScramSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServerProvider.java) handling both SCRAM-SHA-256 and SCRAM-SHA-512 variants via ScramMechanism (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramMechanism.java). ScramServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramServerCallbackHandler.java) manages credential and token validation using CredentialCache, while ScramFormatter (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramFormatter.java) handles SCRAM message formatting and credential generation.\n\n5. **GSSAPI/Kerberos Mechanism**: Kerberos authentication is handled through KerberosLogin (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosLogin.java) and KerberosClientCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosClientCallbackHandler.java). Server-side GSSAPI uses Java's built-in GSSAPI provider. KerberosName (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosName.java) and KerberosShortNamer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosShortNamer.java) handle principal name mapping according to JAAS rules defined in KerberosRule (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosRule.java).\n\n6. **OAuthBearer Mechanism**: OAuthBearerSaslServer (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServer.java) implements OAuth 2.0 token-based authentication with OAuthBearerSaslServerProvider (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServerProvider.java) registering the OAuthBearerSaslServerFactory.\n\n7. **Mechanism Selection Infrastructure**: SaslChannelBuilder (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java) orchestrates the creation of server-side authentication components. It loads JAAS contexts for enabled mechanisms, creates appropriate callback handlers based on the mechanism type, and builds the authenticator. ChannelBuilders (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java) provides factory methods for creating SASL channel builders for both client and server modes.\n\n8. **Callback Handler Infrastructure**: SaslServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerCallbackHandler.java) provides default callback handling for all SASL mechanisms, while mechanism-specific handlers (PlainServerCallbackHandler, ScramServerCallbackHandler) override behavior for their respective mechanisms. AuthenticateCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/AuthenticateCallbackHandler.java) is the interface that all callback handlers must implement.\n\n9. **JAAS Context Management**: JaasContext (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java) and JaasConfig (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasConfig.java) manage JAAS configuration loading and context creation for both static and dynamic JAAS configurations. LoginManager (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/LoginManager.java) manages the lifecycle of JAAS login modules.\n\n10. **Credential Storage**: CredentialCache (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/CredentialCache.java) provides in-memory caching of credentials for SCRAM mechanisms, supporting dynamic credential updates via broker admin operations.\n\nThe authentication flow begins when a client connects to a SASL listener. SaslChannelBuilder builds the server-side channel with appropriate JAAS contexts for all enabled mechanisms. SaslServerAuthenticator receives the initial SASL handshake request, extracts the client's preferred mechanism, creates a SaslServer instance via the appropriate mechanism provider, and proceeds with the SASL token exchange until authentication succeeds or fails. Throughout this process, the mechanism-specific SaslServer implementations handle the cryptographic and validation logic, with callback handlers bridging to the broker's credential stores and authorization rules."
